things.name: The Claddagh & Spanish Arch
things.description: The Claddagh — Ireland's most famous fishing village and origin of the Claddagh ring — beside the medieval Spanish Arch on the Corrib estuary

things.name: Galway City Museum
things.description: Free museum telling the story of Galway from prehistoric times to the present — the city's history, culture and traditions in one place

things.name: Shop Street & Latin Quarter
things.description: Galway's legendary pedestrianised heart — cobbled medieval streets packed with traditional pubs, street performers and independent shops

things.name: Connemara National Park & Kylemore Abbey
things.description: Wild Atlantic bog and mountain landscape around the gorgeous Gothic Kylemore Abbey — Ireland's most romantic building

things.name: Aran Islands (Ferry from Rossaveal)
things.description: Three ancient limestone islands in Galway Bay — Inis Mór with Dún Aonghasa fort, cycling and the timeless Irish-speaking way of life

things.name: Salthill Promenade & Diving Board
things.description: Galway's seafront promenade tradition — kick the wall at the end, swim from the famous diving board and walk the mile-long prom

things.name: Galway Cathedral
things.description: Ireland's last great stone cathedral built in 1965 — a magnificent neoclassical dome rising above the River Corrib

things.name: Cliffs of Moher (Day Trip via Clare)
things.description: Ireland's most visited natural attraction — 214-metre sea cliffs stretching 8km along the Wild Atlantic Way in County Clare

things.name: Dunguaire Castle (Kinvara)
things.description: Iconic 16th-century tower castle on the shores of Galway Bay at Kinvara — medieval banquet evenings in summer

things.name: Galway International Arts Festival (July)
things.description: One of Europe's great arts festivals transforming Galway every July with world-class theatre, music, visual art and street spectacle
